retry_reason: The refcount_inc itself never fails; failures are pre-refcount (allocation or not-found). Retrying may succeed if conditions change (e.g., group appears later), but the refcount operation is not the source of failure.
contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper for multicast group objects. Returns a pointer to an existing group with an incremented refcount, or creates a new group and returns it with an initial refcount of 1 (via kzalloc + atomic_inc). The caller must release the reference by calling a corresponding put function (e.g., release_group) when done. On failure, returns an ERR_PTR; -ENOENT if not found and create=0, -ENOMEM on allocation failure. The function performs full initialization on create, including sysfs attributes, workqueue setup, and list initialization. The caller must check the return value before use.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a reference-counted multicast group (get-or-create). Includes significant initialization logic on the creation path (kzalloc, list/works/sysfs init), but the core semantic is returning a referenced object. The atomic_inc is unconditional on any group returned.

check.reason: The function calls atomic_inc(&group->refcount) on the group object that is returned. The access path group.refcount starts from the returned pointer and contains two segments (group and refcount) matching the two-type chain mcast_group--atomic_t. The operation is consistently a get on the return value.
